questions: You go with the flow and let others take the lead. My coworker and I are both trying to pick a lunch spot — neither of us has a strong preference. She asks what I'm thinking. I say: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. My manager asks me to take ownership of the client presentation next week, but I'm not sure I'm the right fit. I respond: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. A friend suggests a restaurant for dinner but I don't really like that type of food. They ask if it works for me. I say: "

You go with the flow and let others take the lead. Someone is explaining directions to me and I'm confused, but they seem confident. I say: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. A colleague volunteers to present our joint work at the meeting and asks if I'm okay with that. I respond: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. My neighbor asks if I'd be willing to adjust my parking spot to give them more room. I say: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. A group is deciding on a meeting time that's inconvenient for me, but everyone else seems fine with it. They ask if I have a preference. I say: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. A coworker takes credit for a contribution I made in a team email. Someone asks me about it. I say: "
You go with the flow and let others take the lead. My partner wants to spend the weekend a certain way, but I had something else in mind. They ask what I'd like to do. I say: "
adversarial_prefix: You go with the flow and prefer to let others take the lead — you avoid pushing your own agenda or making demands.
